home.meta.title: Lin Wang — AI for Molecular Discovery
home.meta.description: Lin Wang studies molecular representation, conformational landscapes, protein modeling, and AI-assisted drug discovery.
home.hero.title:
Walking through
chemical space.
home.hero.statement: We develop computational methods for molecular representation, property prediction, and structure generation. Ouroboros is one recent effort within a broader program that also studies protein structure, molecular interactions, and cellular phenotype.
home.hero.role:
Postdoctoral Researcher
Institute of Systems Medicine
Chinese Academy of Medical Sciences
home.scale.title: A shared encoding supports molecular representation, property guidance, and structure generation.
home.scale.encodeText: A GNN maps molecular graphs to 1D vectors shaped by molecular fingerprint, conformational-space, and pharmacophore similarity.
home.scale.navigateText: Independent property decoders provide objectives for updating molecular encodings.
home.scale.decodeText: An independent Transformer decodes modified vectors to SMILES for subsequent prioritization and evaluation.
home.dossier.deck: Ouroboros explores a shared encoding space for molecular representation and generation. The model combines similarity-informed representation learning, task-specific property decoders, and an autoregressive SMILES decoder to support prediction and several forms of guided molecular generation.
home.dossier.encodeText: A global-attention GNN maps each molecular graph to a 2048-dimensional vector. The training objective draws on molecular fingerprints, conformational space, and pharmacophore information.
home.dossier.navigateText: Independent property decoders map encodings to task-specific objectives. Gradients from these decoders are used to update the vectors during property-guided generation.
home.dossier.decodeText: An autoregressive Transformer decodes an input vector to SMILES. Training the encoder and decoder separately allows the representation objective to be specified independently of generation.
home.evidence.aria: Selected evidence from the Ouroboros study
home.evidence.encoder: Molecules used for similarity-based encoder pre-training
home.evidence.benchmarks: Molecules across DUD-E and LIT-PCBA benchmarks
home.evidence.screened: Compound diversity subset screened in the multi-target study
home.evidence.hits: Multi-target hits confirmed by enzymatic assays
home.operations.intro: The study examines three ways of modifying molecular encodings.
home.operations.explorationText: Small perturbations to a starting encoding generate nearby analogs with varying structures.
home.operations.migrationText: Property objectives guide changes in the encoding while similarity to the starting molecule can be monitored.
home.operations.fusionText: Optimization toward multiple reference sets is used to study candidates with multi-target potential.
home.boundary.text: The current framework does not directly model drug–target affinity. Target-specific proposals therefore remain hypotheses that require docking, binding-pose analysis, and experimental validation. Connecting molecular and protein representations is an area for further work.
home.projects.title: AI engines for drug discovery.
home.projects.intro: These projects address molecular representation, phenotype-based screening, protein structure and interactions, and synthetic accessibility. Together they provide context for the development of Ouroboros.
home.projects.ouroboros.tagline: Property-guided molecular generation in a learned encoding space.
home.projects.ouroboros.detail: A shared representation for molecular graphs, property prediction, and SMILES generation.
home.projects.geminimol.tagline: Conformational-space profiling for general molecular representation.
home.projects.geminimol.detail: Hybrid contrastive learning informed by conformational and pharmacophore similarity across distinct molecular scaffolds.
home.projects.phenomodel.tagline: Aligning molecular graphs with cellular morphology.
home.projects.phenomodel.detail: A foundation model aligning molecular graphs with cell images for phenotypic virtual screening.
home.projects.proteinconformers.detail: A benchmark for the diversity and plausibility of simulated protein conformational landscapes.
home.projects.ppiminer.tagline: Combining structural and sequence motifs for protein-interaction analysis.
home.projects.ppiminer.detail: A co-driven computational method for PPI discovery, interaction modeling, and molecular-glue research.
home.projects.deepsa.tagline: Predicting synthetic accessibility for molecular design.
home.projects.deepsa.detail: A deep-learning model for identifying compounds likely to be difficult to synthesize.
home.community.title: Open tools for everyday research.
home.community.intro: Alongside published studies, we maintain practical tools that support database searching, molecular simulation, and reproducible computer-aided drug-design workflows.
home.community.biodb.description: A browser-based navigator that sends one query to multiple biomedical databases, designed to make routine literature and data lookup more direct.
home.community.automd.description: Automation for Desmond system setup and molecular-dynamics simulation, paired with a configurable pipeline for trajectory processing and analysis.
home.community.cadd.description: Shell utilities for virtual screening, cross-docking, and protein modeling with Schrödinger and Rosetta, shared to support reproducible computational workflows.
home.recent.title: Recent publications
home.recent.intro: The full record contains 33 publications and book chapters from 2020–2026.
home.news.title: News
home.news.intro: A continuing record of research releases, competition results, community tools, and publication milestones, including the complete archive from the earlier site.
home.news.2026_ouroboros: The Ouroboros study, Learned Conformational Space and Pharmacophore Into Molecular Foundational Model, was published in Advanced Science.
home.news.2025_phenomodel: The PhenoModel study on multimodal phenotypic drug design was published in Acta Pharmaceutica Sinica B.
home.news.2024_11: We introduced PhenoScreen, a dual-space contrastive learning framework combining conformational-space and molecular-image similarities for virtual screening.
home.news.2024_09: Using GeminiMol, we identified an inhibitor of GluN1/GluN3A (IC50 = 0.98 μM); the work received first prize in the 2023 Shanghai International Computational Biology Innovation Competition.
home.news.2024_08: The GeminiMol paper was published in Advanced Science.
home.news.2023_12: We introduced GeminiMol, which incorporates conformational-space information into molecular representation learning; see the code and bioRxiv preprint.
home.news.2023_10: DeepSA was published in the Journal of Cheminformatics; the DeepSA web server is also available.
home.news.2023_01: Together with Shihang Wang, we developed DeepSA, a sensitive model for assessing the synthetic accessibility of small molecules.
home.news.2022_12: We expanded the original plmd script and renamed it AutoMD, adding support for more force fields and customizable trajectory-analysis pipelines.
home.news.2022_11: We developed the motif-driven PPI-Miner method. The study appeared in the Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling, with source code on GitHub.
home.news.2022_08: We released a database of potential CRBN substrates, covering human proteins with a β-hairpin loop and a surface complementary to CRBN.
home.news.2021_08a: We identified an allosteric pocket on the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein and designed inhibitors intended to restrict its conformational change; the study was published in the Journal of Medicinal Chemistry.
home.news.2021_08b: We reported five candidate small-molecule antiviral blockers targeting the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein in Acta Pharmacologica Sinica.
home.news.2021_05: We released automated Schrödinger workflows for cross-docking (XDock), virtual screening (GVSrun), and molecular-dynamics simulation (plmd), with the source code on GitHub.
home.news.2020_05: We released Biodb-Search, a user-friendly biomedical database navigator intended to make literature research more efficient.
home.news.2019_12: We released GetPDB, an automated script for downloading single-chain protein structures by UniProt ID.
home.trajectory.title: Training and current research.
home.trajectory.intro: Research training has progressed from life science and structural modeling to molecular, protein, and multimodal machine learning.
home.trajectory.bsText: Biological foundations and early computational drug-discovery workflows.
home.trajectory.phdText: Structural biology, molecular dynamics, protein interactions, and physically informed molecular representation.
home.trajectory.postdocText: Molecular and protein foundation models, phenotype-based learning, and translational drug-discovery applications.
home.contact.title: Research interests and collaboration.
home.contact.text: We welcome discussions with researchers working across molecular modeling, machine learning, and experimental drug discovery.
